english: just now; only just; barely; firm; hard

etymology.description: phonetic 岡 (gāng) + semantic 刀 (knife) -> hard, firm
etymology.interpretation: 岡(岡) (gāng, 'mountain ridge') provides the sound, suggesting a firm or solid structure, with 刀(刀) (dāo, 'knife') indicating sharpness or firmness. Thus, 刚(剛) (gāng, 'firm') connotes hardness or strength, while the definite, exact nature of a firm edge lends to meanings of precise timing ('just now') and minimal sufficiency ('barely').
etymology.simplification: 剛(剛) (gāng, 'firm') was simplified to 刚(剛) by replacing the phonetic 岡(岡) (gāng, 'mountain ridge') with 冈(冈), retaining the knife radical 刀(刀).

examples.chinese: 我刚吃完晚饭(我剛吃完晚飯)。
他刚走进教室(他剛走進教室)，铃声就响了(鈴聲就響了)。
examples.pinyin: Tā gāng zǒu jìn jiàoshì, língshēng jiù xiǎng le.
examples.english: He just entered the classroom when the bell rang.

examples.chinese: 这个盒子很坚硬(這個盒子很堅硬)，像石头一样(像石頭一樣)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège hézi hěn jiānyìng, xiàng shítou yīyàng.
examples.english: This box is very hard, like a stone.
